---
title: "IBM X31\Ubuntu 11.04 the unity wont start and adobe flash slow"
date: 2011-08-06
forum: Hardware
---

### Post by herokiller on 2011-08-06
Dear All,
I got my laptop installed Ubuntu 11.04 few days ago but i could not get the unity started and the adobe flash player lagging. I also tried few game and one of them (blob wars) freeze after I actually enter the game. (it did not freeze in the beginning when I launch the game)
My xorg.conf as following:

---

### Post by SalHelder on 2011-08-07
To be sure, does your computer fit with these specs?


Intel Pentium M 1.3 GHz Processor
256MB PC2100 DDR Memory
20 Gigabyte Hard Drive
12.1" XGA TFT Display and ATI Radeon Mobility Graphics with 16MB Memory

If so, it's perfectly normal that Unity doesn't load or even won't work anything close to usable, I'd recommend you to try XFCE/Xubuntu as a desktop because you really don't have enough memory to work around with heavier desktop environments such as GNOME, Unity or KDE.

About flash, that's perfectly normal, you'll need a quite powerful computer to work with flash properly. But you can try using a lightweight browser under XFCE like Epiphany or Midori and then see if it improves. And in this case blocking flash/animated ads is highly recommended.

---

### Post by riedel on 2011-10-23
> **SalHelder said:**
> 
Intel Pentium M 1.3 GHz Processor
256MB PC2100 DDR Memory
20 Gigabyte Hard Drive
12.1" XGA TFT Display and ATI Radeon Mobility Graphics with 16MB Memory

If so, it's perfectly normal that Unity doesn't load or even won't work anything close to usable, I'd recommend you to try XFCE/Xubuntu as a desktop because you really don't have enough memory to work around with heavier desktop environments such as GNOME, Unity or KDE.
